2606.24820.title: SHERLOC: Structured Diagnostic Localization for Code Repair Agents
2606.24820.paper_summary: LLM agents solve repository-level coding tasks through multi-turn tool use, but utilize half their budget on locating faults before editing. Dedicated localization frameworks have emerged, yet are still evaluated as file retrieval rather than actionable diagnosis, producing locations without the diagnostic context a repair agent needs. We introduce SHERLOC (Structured Hypothesis-driven Exploration and Reasoning for Localization), a training-free framework pairing a reasoning LLM with compact repository tools and self-recovery, without fine-tuning or multi-agent orchestration. SHERLOC reaches state-of-the-art localization across model scales: 84.33% accuracy@1 on SWE-Bench Lite and 81.27% recall@1 on SWE-Bench Verified; at ~30B parameters, it matches or outperforms other agentic methods. Injecting our locations and diagnostic findings into repair agents yields, on average, +5.95 pp resolve rate on SWE-Bench Verified while cutting localization and total tokens by 36.7% and 23.1%.
2606.24820.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理透過多輪工具使用解決庫級編碼任務，但在編輯之前將一半的預算用於定位故障。專門的本地化框架已經出現，但仍然被評估為文件檢索，而非可行的診斷，產生的位置缺乏修復代理所需的診斷上下文。我們介紹 SHERLOC（結構化假設驅動的探索與本地化推理），這是一個無需訓練的框架，將推理 LLM 與緊湊的庫工具和自我恢復配對，無需微調或多代理協調。SHERLOC 在模型規模上達到最先進的本地化：在 SWE-Bench Lite 上的準確率為 84.33% @1，在 SWE-Bench Verified 上的召回率為 81.27% @1；在約 30B 參數下，它與其他代理方法相匹配或超越。將我們的位置和診斷結果注入修復代理，平均提高了 SWE-Bench Verified 上 +5.95 個百分點的解決率，同時將本地化和總標記數減少了 36.7% 和 23.1%。
2606.24820.author: Hovhannes Tamoyan et.al.
2606.24820.authors: Hovhannes Tamoyan, Sean Narenthiran, Erik Arakelyan, Mira Mezini, Boris Ginsburg
